text.title: product-led-growth Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for product-led-growth as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Activation metric drift: "Activation" gets redefined every quarter to make the chart go up. Without a frozen, written definition tied to retention, the number is decorative.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A metric that changes definition each quarter is decorative; the trend line means nothing because the denominator changed.

failure-mode.description: Freemium cannibalization: Free tier eats paid usage when limits are wrong; PMs lack the pricing instrumentation to detect it (need cohort revenue, not signup count).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Track cohort revenue separately; throttling on usage that correlates with willingness-to-pay, not on signup count.

failure-mode.description: PQL → sales hand-off rot: PQL definition is owned by PM, scoring lives in product analytics, but acted on by sales in CRM. Three systems out of sync equals silent loss of deals.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Route via webhook to CRM SDR queue, not Slack; create a durable, auditable record.

failure-mode.description: One-size-fits-all onboarding: PLG playbooks assume one ICP; in reality multiple personas hit signup and a single tour fails all of them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Segment onboarding paths by persona; personalize from first signup.

failure-mode.description: Vanity North Star: "Weekly active users" without a revenue link rewards engagement hacks (notifications, streaks) that hurt retention long-term.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Link every metric to revenue impact; optimize for NRR and D30 retention, not just DAU/WAU.

failure-mode.description: Time-to-value cliff: TTV less than 5 min only matters if the second session happens. PMs over-optimize first-run and lose D7 retention.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: D1 to D7 conversion is the real growth lever once TTV is sub-10-min.

failure-mode.description: PLG equals no marketing: Without content/SEO funnel, PLG equals empty funnel; PMs ship features instead of fixing acquisition and blame product-market fit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: PLG is not acquisition; without inbound traffic the activation funnel optimizes an empty pipe.

failure-mode.description: Ownership ambiguity: Activation owned by PM, conversion owned by growth, retention shared with CS — gaps between handoffs are where churn lives.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Define clear hand-off contracts and owners for each stage of the funnel.

failure-mode.description: LLM "activation rate is great" hallucination: Agents will confidently restate a number without checking the time window.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force the agent to print the SQL/HogQL it ran and the row count it received before any commentary.

failure-mode.description: Drift between PQL spec and SQL: Agent edits the YAML but not the dbt model, or vice versa — funnel silently changes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CI must verify spec-yaml hash matches model annotation.

failure-mode.description: Funnel-drop hypothesis collapse: Single-agent runs converge on "improve onboarding copy."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use 3 agents with separate roles (data, customer voice, competitor) and force divergent hypotheses before merging.

failure-mode.description: Survivorship bias on retention cohorts: Agent reports "D30 retention is 60%" but excludes cohorts that have not aged 30 days.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force explicit cohort-age filter in every retention query.

failure-mode.description: PQL inflation: Agent loosens PQL thresholds because count is too low.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Threshold changes are governance events; require a git diff review on plg-definitions.yml, never accept inline tweaks.

failure-mode.description: Expansion attribution: Agent credits expansion to the last feature shipped; usage growth has multi-week lag.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use a fixed attribution window per the spec, not "what feels recent."

failure-mode.description: Hand-off prompt-injection risk: PQL spec lives in a YAML the sales agent reads; if untrusted user content lands there, it can hijack the sales action.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Treat the file as a contract, validate schema before any agent ingest.

failure-mode.description: Confidentiality leak in cohort exports: Cohort dumps include emails, plan tier, sometimes free-form survey text.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Run password-scrubber-agent before sharing analytics output externally or with broad-access bots.
